findings.description: Mechanism of Action (MOA) claims for LISRAYA, specifically relating to targeted root cause and turning off specific inflammatory switches, are missing from the candidate rules.

payload.description: Approved verbatim mechanism of action (MOA) claims must be used when describing how LISRAYA works to block specific inflammatory signals and target root cause rather than broad immune suppression.

findings.description: The headline constraint 'YOU DESERVE MORE. So there's LISRAYA—the first targeted treatment for dermatomyositis' contains clinical and comparative claims (e.g., 'first targeted treatment for dermatomyositis') which require medical/legal approval before dissemination, but the rule lacks governance parameters.
